2606.12199.title: Which Speech Representation Better Matches Text-Native Reasoning? A Study of Speech-Text Alignment on Frame Rate and Representation
2606.12199.paper_summary: Spoken dialogue models typically start from text LLM backbones, yet reasoning often degrades when conditioning on speech instead of text. We attribute part of this modality gap to a temporal-granularity mismatch: speech tokens are temporally redundant and far longer than text under matched semantics, diluting per-token semantic density and weakening text-native reasoning dynamics. We study speech token design as a representation selection problem and sweep frame rates under a frozen LLM backbone with a fixed information rate. To make low frame rates feasible, we introduce factorized FSQ and a lightweight non-autoregressive audio LM head, scaling capacity to nearly 300\,bits/frame without sacrificing efficient prediction. With the bottleneck removed, we sweep frame rates (50$\rightarrow$2.08\,Hz) and alignment depth, and observe a consistent best regime for speech QA at 4.17\,Hz with intermediate-layer representation alignment.
2606.12199.paper_summary_zh: 口語對話模型通常以文本 LLM 骨幹為起點，但當以語音而非文本為條件時，推理往往會下降。我們將這種模態差距的一部分歸因於時間粒度的不匹配：語音標記在時間上是冗餘的，且在語義匹配的情況下比文本長得多，稀釋了每個標記的語義密度，並削弱了文本原生的推理動態。我們將語音標記設計視為一種表示選擇問題，並在固定信息率的情況下，對凍結的 LLM 骨幹進行幀率掃描。為了使低幀率可行，我們引入了分解的 FSQ 和輕量級的非自回歸音頻 LM 頭，將容量擴展至近 300\,位元/幀，而不犧牲高效預測。隨著瓶頸的消除，我們掃描幀率（50$\rightarrow$2.08\,Hz）和對齊深度，並觀察到在 4.17\,Hz 的語音 QA 中，具有中間層表示對齊的一致最佳狀態。
2606.12199.author: Zhen Ye et.al.
2606.12199.authors: Zhen Ye, Xu Tan, Yiming Li, Guangyan Zhang, Chimin Chan, Haohe Liu, Zhengxi Liu, Hongzhan Lin, Zheqi Dai, Xinshen Zhang, Peiwen Sun, Qiuqiang Kong, Wei Xue
